diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Monument. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: category - Example triple: (11th Mississippi Infantry Monument | category | Contributing property)
Predicate: country - Example triple: (11th Mississippi Infantry Monument | country | "United States")
Predicate: location - Example triple: (11th Mississippi Infantry Monument | location | Adams County, Pennsylvania)
Predicate: municipality - Example triple: (11th Mississippi Infantry Monument | municipality | Gettysburg, Pennsylvania)
Predicate: state - Example triple: (11th Mississippi Infantry Monument | state | "Pennsylvania")
Predicate: district - Example triple: (14th New Jersey Volunteer Infantry Monument | district | Monocacy National Battlefield)
Predicate: established - Example triple: (14th New Jersey Volunteer Infantry Monument | established | "1907-07-11")
Predicate: foundingDate - Example triple: (14th New Jersey Volunteer Infantry Monument | foundingDate | 1907-07-11)
Predicate: owner - Example triple: (14th New Jersey Volunteer Infantry Monument | owner | National Park Service)
Predicate: owningOrganisation - Example triple: (14th New Jersey Volunteer Infantry Monument | owningOrganisation | National Park Service)
Predicate: hasToItsNorth - Example triple: (Adams County, Pennsylvania | hasToItsNorth | Cumberland County, Pennsylvania)
Predicate: hasToItsSouthwest - Example triple: (Adams County, Pennsylvania | hasToItsSouthwest | Frederick County, Maryland)
Predicate: hasToItsWest - Example triple: (Adams County, Pennsylvania | hasToItsWest | Franklin County, Pennsylvania)
Predicate: inaugurationDate - Example triple: (Atatürk Monument (İzmir) | inaugurationDate | "1932-07-27")
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Azerbaijan | capital | Baku)
Predicate: legislature - Example triple: (Azerbaijan | legislature | National Assembly (Azerbaijan))
Predicate: dedicatedTo - Example triple: (Baku Turkish Martyrs' Memorial | dedicatedTo | "Ottoman Army soldiers killed in the Battle of Baku")
Predicate: designer - Example triple: (Baku Turkish Martyrs' Memorial | designer | "Hüseyin Bütüner and Hilmi Güner")
Predicate: material - Example triple: (Dead Man's Plack | material | Rock (geology))
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (England | ethnicGroup | British Arabs)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (England | language | Cornish language)
Predicate: religion - Example triple: (England | religion | Church of England)
Predicate: nearestCity - Example triple: (Monocacy National Battlefield | nearestCity | Frederick, Maryland)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (Turkey | currency | Turkish lira)
Predicate: largestCity - Example triple: (Turkey | largestCity | Istanbul)
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Turkey | leader | Ahmet Davutoğlu)
Predicate: leaderTitle - Example triple: (Turkey | leaderTitle | President of Turkey)
Predicate: hasToItsSoutheast - Example triple: (Adams County, Pennsylvania | hasToItsSoutheast | Carroll County, Maryland)
Predicate: nativeName - Example triple: (Baku Turkish Martyrs' Memorial | nativeName | "Türk Şehitleri Anıtı")

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a monument (or related entity) into a single
    fluent sentence. All triples are combined; each predicate is expressed with a
    suitable phrase. Handles related entities (like country properties) using relative clauses.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    triples_by_subject: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in triples_by_subject:
            triples_by_subject[triple.subject] = []
        triples_by_subject[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Main subject is the monument (first triple's subject)
    subject = triples[0].subject
    main_triples = triples_by_subject.get(subject, [])
    other_subjects = [s for s in triples_by_subject.keys() if s != subject]

    # Build the main sentence about the monument
    sentence_parts: list[str] = []

    # Start with the subject
    sentence_parts.append(subject)

    # Track which predicates we've processed
    processed_predicates: set[str] = set()

    # Process main subject predicates
    for triple in main_triples:
        p = triple.predicate
        o = triple.object.strip('"')  # remove surrounding quotes if present
        processed_predicates.add(p)

        # Map predicates to natural-language fragments
        if p == "category":
            sentence_parts.append(f"is a {o}")
        elif p == "country":
            sentence_parts.append(f"is located in {o}")
        elif p == "location":
            sentence_parts.append(f"located in {o}")
        elif p == "municipality":
            sentence_parts.append(f"in the municipality of {o}")
        elif p == "state":
            sentence_parts.append(f"in the state of {o}")
        elif p == "district":
            sentence_parts.append(f"within the district of {o}")
        elif p in ("established", "foundingDate"):
            sentence_parts.append(f"was established on {o}")
        elif p in ("owner", "owningOrganisation"):
            sentence_parts.append(f"owned by {o}")
        elif p == "inaugurationDate":
            sentence_parts.append(f"was inaugurated on {o}")
        elif p == "dedicatedTo":
            sentence_parts.append(f"dedicated to {o}")
        elif p == "designer":
            sentence_parts.append(f"designed by {o}")
        elif p == "material":
            sentence_parts.append(f"made of {o}")
        elif p == "nativeName":
            sentence_parts.append(f"known in its native language as {o}")
        elif p == "nearestCity":
            sentence_parts.append(f"near {o}")
        elif p == "hasToItsNorth":
            sentence_parts.append(f"has {o} to its north")
        elif p == "hasToItsSouthwest":
            sentence_parts.append(f"has {o} to its southwest")
        elif p == "hasToItsWest":
            sentence_parts.append(f"has {o} to its west")
        elif p == "hasToItsSoutheast":
            sentence_parts.append(f"has {o} to its southeast")
        else:
            # Skip predicates that belong to other subjects
            continue

    # Build the main sentence with proper grammar
    if len(sentence_parts) <= 1:
        # If only subject, try to find any predicate we could use
        if main_triples:
            first_triple = main_triples[0]
            o = first_triple.object.strip('"')
            if first_triple.predicate == "country":
                return f"{subject} is located in {o}."
            elif first_triple.predicate == "location":
                return f"{subject} is located in {o}."
            elif first_triple.predicate == "inaugurationDate":
                return f"{subject} was inaugurated on {o}."
            elif first_triple.predicate == "designer":
                return f"{subject} was designed by {o}."
            elif first_triple.predicate == "material":
                return f"{subject} is made of {o}."
            else:
                return f"{subject} {first_triple.predicate} {o}."
        return subject

    main_sentence = sentence_parts[0]
    if len(sentence_parts) == 2:
        main_sentence += " " + sentence_parts[1] + "."
    else:
        # Join all but last with commas, add "and" before last
        main_sentence += " " + ", ".join(sentence_parts[1:-1]) + ", and " + sentence_parts[-1] + "."

    # Add information about related entities (e.g., country properties) using relative clauses
    if other_subjects:
        for other_subject in other_subjects:
            other_triples = triples_by_subject[other_subject]
            other_clauses: list[str] = []

            for triple in other_triples:
                p = triple.predicate
                o = triple.object.strip('"')

                if p == "capital":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the capital is {o}")
                elif p == "largestCity":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the largest city is {o}")
                elif p == "leader":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the leader is {o}")
                elif p == "leaderTitle":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the leader title is {o}")
                elif p == "currency":
                    other_clauses.append(f"uses {o} as currency")
                elif p == "language":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the language is {o}")
                elif p == "religion":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the religion is {o}")
                elif p == "ethnicGroup":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the ethnic group includes {o}")
                elif p == "legislature":
                    other_clauses.append(f"the legislature is {o}")
                elif p == "capital":
                    # Avoid saying "capital is X" if we already mentioned the country
                    other_clauses.append(f"the capital is {o}")
                elif p == "currency":
                    other_clauses.append(f"uses {o} as currency")
                else:
                    other_clauses.append(f"{p.replace('_', ' ')}: {o}")

            if other_clauses:
                # Create relative clause for the related entity
                # Check if we already mentioned this entity to avoid redundancy
                if other_subject in main_sentence:
                    # Entity already mentioned, add info directly
                    if len(other_clauses) == 1:
                        relative_clause = f" ({other_clauses[0]})"
                    else:
                        relative_clause = f" ({', '.join(other_clauses[:-1])}, and {other_clauses[-1]})"
                else:
                    # Entity not mentioned yet, use "which is in" construction
                    if len(other_clauses) == 1:
                        relative_clause = f" which is in {other_subject} ({other_clauses[0]})"
                    else:
                        relative_clause = f" which is in {other_subject} ({', '.join(other_clauses[:-1])}, and {other_clauses[-1]})"
                main_sentence = main_sentence.rstrip('.') + relative_clause + "."

    return main_sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

# Task
Suggest an improvement to the current program that will improve the combined_score.
Different solutions with similar combined_score score but different ideas are valuable.
Creating one complex sentence from all the given triples will award greater score as well.

You MUST use the exact SEARCH/REPLACE diff format shown below to indicate changes:

=======

>>>>>>> REPLACE

The SEARCH section must exactly match the code in the current program, even with it's indentations and endlines.
Do not provide explanations, only the SEARCH/REPLACE section.
IMPORTANT: Do not rewrite the entire program - focus on targeted improvements.
